name: Islamic Center of Irvine
mission: Mission statement - To establish a comprehensive center promoting Islamic values and reflecting excellence in serving the needs of the community
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2024 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.
zakatClaimEvidence: The Islamic Center of Irvine explicitly accepts zakat donations. Their website's 'Donate' page includes a section for 'Zakat & Sadaqah'. Furthermore, their 'Financial Assistance' page states, 'If you would like to contribute funds to help needy families you can do so by either donating your Zakat ul-Maal or your Sadaqah. Please click the corresponding link below. Jazaakumullaahu khayran! Click Here To Donate Zakat Ul-Maal'. An 'ICOI Zakat Fund' is also mentioned, with 100% of the fund going towards 'zakat eligible members in the community'. (Source: https://icoi.net)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 26 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

dimension_explanations.alignment: As a zakat-eligible institution, the center provides a medium fit for Muslim donors seeking to support religious and cultural causes [1]. Its mission emphasizes excellence in serving community needs, though the urgency is categorized as religious-cultural rather than emergency-based [1][2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building within the community, allocating 89.9% of its $2,209,994 revenue to its core programs [2]. While financial health is robust with 26.3 months of working capital, there is limited publicly available evidence regarding specific cost-per-beneficiary outcomes [1][2].

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 89.9%</cite>, ensuring that most funds directly support its mission.
ICOI spends <cite id="4">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite>, allowing virtually all donations to be directed toward community needs.

strengths_deep_dive: The Islamic Center of Irvine demonstrates a strong capacity for financial management and community reinvestment. Its <cite id="2">revenue of over $2.2 million</cite> is supported by a diverse base, including <cite id="8">$1,944,081 in direct contributions</cite> and <cite id="9">$206,424 in program service revenue</cite>. This financial health is further bolstered by a <cite id="10">100% independent board of directors</cite>, which ensures objective oversight of the organization's strategic direction and grantmaking activities, such as the <cite id="5">$2.1 million distributed to domestic partners</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Center of Irvine Inc (ICOI) serves as a vital community hub in Southern California, dedicated to <cite id="1">promoting Islamic values and reflecting excellence</cite> in serving the local Muslim population. As a religious and cultural institution, ICOI provides a wide array of services ranging from spiritual guidance to community support. In the 2022 fiscal year, the organization demonstrated significant financial scale, reporting <cite id="2">total revenue of $2,209,994</cite>. A defining characteristic of ICOI's operations is its commitment to direct service delivery, as evidenced by a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 89.9%</cite>. This high ratio indicates that the vast majority of donor contributions are utilized for core community programming rather than administrative overhead. Furthermore, the organization maintains <cite id="4">exceptional fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, suggesting that its growth is driven by organic community support rather than costly marketing campaigns. Beyond its internal operations, ICOI acts as a significant local benefactor; in 2022, it distributed <cite id="5">$2,125,949 in total grants</cite> to domestic recipients, including substantial support for the <cite id="6">Rahma Center</cite>. This grantmaking activity positions the organization as a central pillar of the regional Islamic infrastructure, facilitating the growth of affiliated community services.
